dialogue.utterance: Unit 3, respond for a reported Stab/Gunshot Wound/Penetrating Trauma at Central Plaza. Call type is penetrating trauma with unknown weapon.
Hi, I'm Jordan, a paramedic with the rescue squad. What brought you to call 911 today?
The patient was stabbed in the lower left quadrant with evisceration. MOI supports penetrating injury, evisceration observed on arrival.
Ma'am, can you open your eyes for me?
Yeah… Kim. She opens eyes to verbal command post arrival.
What date is it today?
Maybe the 14th. Per the ePCR, she correctly responds to orientation during AVPU.
Where are we? Provider notes orientation is mildly decreased (GCS 13).
[CITY]… maybe. Her GCS is 13 during on-scene evaluation.
Can you squeeze my fingers for me?
Yeah, I can do that. Motor response is consistent with GCS 6.
Airway is patent and clear of secretions. As per the ePCR, airway is patent and undisturbed.
Your breathing seems unlabored and normal.
Respirations at 20, unlabored. According to documentation at 01:29, this is the first vital recorded.
Pulse is 103, regular. First assessment notes tachycardia without dysrhythmia.
Let me check your oxygen saturation now.
Your oxygen saturation is 78%. Per ePCR, initial SPO2 is 78% on room air.
Blood pressure is 128 over 71, normotensive.
Attempting blood glucose reading… no result recorded.
There was no reading obtained. No result was documented per the ePCR.
Your skin is normal in color for your ethnicity, no cyanosis observed.
No jugular venous distention detected. Per the ePCR, JVD is negative.
Trachea is midline, no deviation present at this time.
Applying 4-lead EKG. As per the ePCR, 4-lead monitoring is initiated per protocol.
Showing sinus tach with no ectopy. As per ePCR, EKG is in sinus tachycardia.
When did this injury occur?
About 10 minutes before you arrived. As stated in the ePCR narrative.
Did you lose consciousness at any point?
No, but I feel really nauseated and might vomit. Denies LOC, admits nausea as per ePCR.
How would you rate your pain on a scale of 0 to 10?
I don't feel much pain at all. Per ePCR, no pain rated.
Are you allergic to any medications or foods?
No known drug allergies. Per the ePCR, patient states this.
Are you currently on any medications?
No regularly taken medications.
Have you had any major health issues or surgeries?
No major surgeries. Had tetanus shot within five years. Per ePCR, patient reports past tetanus exposure.
When was the last time you had anything to eat or drink?
I had some drinks earlier, not sure how long ago. Based on ePCR, patient admits to alcohol consumption.
Can you tell me what happened before the injury?
I was outside a club and got jumped. As stated in the ePCR narrative.
Observing abdominal evisceration in the lower left quadrant. Per ePCR, this is confirmed.
Strong odor of ethanol on breath. Per the ePCR, this is documented.
Your skin is warm and moist, no signs of abnormal turgor. Skin is described as normal for ethnicity in ePCR.
Lung sounds are clear and equal bilaterally. Per ePCR, no abnormal breath sounds.
IV is being flushed and monitored. Per ePCR, IV started as per protocol.
Officer is applying direct pressure to the wound. Direct pressure noted as continuing in document.
Abdominal contents are covered with a saline-soaked sterile dressing. Per ePCR, protrusions were covered with sterile dressing.
Your last tetanus shot was within the last 5 years. As stated in the narrative.
Did you use any drugs recently?
I used something, but I don't remember exactly what. As stated in ePCR.
You're aware of where you are but might be a bit confused. Provider notes altered mental status and GCS 13.
Let me check your vitals again.
Your pulse is now 110, still tachycardic. Second set of vitals obtained at 01:33:35.
Respiration rate has decreased to 14. Per 01:33:35 vitals.
Systolic is now 104, diastolic 68. Per ePCR, BP falls slightly from prior point.
Oxygen level improved to 96%. Levels improved from previous measurement.
Still observing sinus tach, no premature beats. Per 01:33:35 ePCR.
I cannot take your temperature on scene, but your skin feels warm for now.
Protrusion is in the lower left quadrant, no bleeding observed currently.
CV system is now active and recording. Per ePCR, continuous monitoring applied.
You are going directly to [COMPANY_NAME] trauma bay. Destination is as noted in the narrative.
You will be transported in a fully reclined position. Supine position is needed for evisceration case.
Trauma team is aware and awaiting treatment. Pre-alert to [COMPANY_NAME] as stated in ePCR.
Seat belt secured for transport safety. Per ePCR, seat belts applied.
You will be handed directly to the trauma team upon arrival. Per ePCR.
We will assist [COMPANY_NAME] staff with immediate transfer. This was done as per ePCR.
This was standard trauma protocol, field impression matched MOI. Per ePCR, protocol followed for treatment.
Handoff to trauma team is smooth and without incident. Per ePCR, no problems noted during handoff.
No blood glucose was obtained in the field, as reflected in the ePCR.
Unable to obtain personal information on scene or at ED. EPCR notes no demographics obtained.
You were ideal with 128 systolic earlier, but now at 104, this is mild hypotension per field vitals.
Oxygen saturation was 78% initially, which is mild hypoxia. Now improved to 96%.
This happened about 10 minutes prior to arrival, still within narrow window for abdominal injury.
Skin is pale to cool now, need to monitor for signs of shock.
This case is handled using protocol injury - general trauma management (protocol 4-1), as found in ePCR.
You said no pain, but we will monitor for increase in discomfort during transport. As per ePCR.
No demographic information is available from on scene or arrival. As per ePCR.
You are being transported with full gowns and gloves for safety. Per ePCR.
We anticipate minor delays at the hospital for paperwork and decontamination. Per ePCR.
No personal items found with patient during on-scene or ED assessment. As noted in the ePCR.
Transfer to trauma team is done with no issues. Trauma team is now in charge.
I'll check the pulse again. It's regular and strong in all limbs. Per ePCR, peripheral pulses were strong and regular.
Sats are steady at 96%-keeping the mask on during transport. Patient on room air as documented.
You are secured and ready for transport now. Movement will be smooth and controlled.
We are monitoring your vitals continuously per protocol. Will stay on EKG and IV.
Provider assets are available on arrival-[COMPANY_NAME] team is expecting you now.
I will stay with you all the way to their team consent. No handoff before trauma team.
Receiving transport consent and initiating move now. As per ePCR.
Airway secure, all procedures completed. Ready for the transport.
[COMPANY_NAME], EMS Team Unit 3 rolling. Code incoming.
EMS in control-anterior transport in control of driver seat.
Seat properly loaded with EMS in control of speed and stability.
Emergency exit staged-will enter [COMPANY_NAME] with paramedic leading.
Lateral approach as per trauma bay instructions received earlier.
Full grasp of patient secured during load, no movement observed.
EMS movement to trauma team now complete without complications.
Care transition complete, [COMPANY_NAME] staff now in charge as per protocol.
Transport concluded, care handed off smoothly. No issues reported.
